logic_gaps.description: No CP-NNN scenario covers legacy state migration from scratch/ to review/ artifact tree, despite this being a documented contract in start-review-loop.md and the YAML workflow (auto + confirm). CP suite has 6 scenarios but skips the migration boundary that the workflow handles at init.

No CP-NNN scenario covers session classification (resume / restart / completed-session) lifecycle modes, despite the YAML workflow having explicit classify-session steps. The CP suite focuses on artifact-shape contracts, not lifecycle-state contracts.

No CP-NNN scenario covers stuck detection and recovery flow, despite the YAML workflow having stuck-recovery branches. CP suite has resource-map gate (CP-054) and write boundary (CP-057) but no scenario exercises the stuck-recovery path.

novel_findings: Direction-A (CP scenarios testing missing contracts) is CLEAN: all 6 CP-052..057 scenarios test contracts actually present in command + YAML surfaces. Strong validation that the CP suite is well-aligned.

Direction-B finding pattern: CP suite covers 6 artifact-shape contracts but skips 3 lifecycle/migration contracts (state migration, session classification, stuck recovery). All P2 because they're test-suite-coverage gaps, not functional bugs.
Iter 7 surfaced 3 novel gaps + 4 duplicates of LG-0012/0013/0014/0015. Convergence: 7 of 10 iters complete, duplicates increasing as expected. Iters 8-10 likely to plateau further.
notes: Iter 7 RQ: CP-052..057 command-flow stress tests vs command/YAML drift. Wall-clock 130s. Direction-A clean (no CP scenarios test missing contracts). Direction-B surfaced 3 novel CP-coverage gaps (LG-0028/0029/0030, all P2 test-suite-coverage shortfalls). 4 duplicates of prior LG-#### gaps (extended gates, executor variants, pause sentinel, graph convergence). Saturation deepening as expected.
